role: Senior IRGC commander; close associate of Hassan Tehrani Moghaddam
bio: Mostafa Izadi is a senior commander in the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) who served alongside [Hassan Tehrani Moghaddam](/people/hassan-tehrani-moghaddam) in the early years of Iran's ballistic missile programme. Izadi testified that Moghaddam pioneered the IRGC's ground-to-ground missile systems from 1984, beginning with the Oghab rocket during the Iran–Iraq War. He has been cited as one of Moghaddam's closest colleagues and provided first-hand accounts of the foundational period of Iran's indigenous missile development effort.
editorial_notes: Izadi testified publicly that Moghaddam developed ground-to-ground missile systems from 1984. Biographical details are sparse in open sources; role and faction inferred from IRGC affiliation and context.
